what-is-ct.title: What is a CT Scan?
what-is-ct.description: Learn what happens inside the machine

prepare.title: How to Prepare
prepare.description: What to wear, remove, and expect

staying-still.title: Staying Still
staying-still.description: Why it matters for your scan quality

video.noVideo: This video is not yet available

what-is-ct: Uses X-rays to create detailed cross-section images of your body
The table moves slowly through a large ring — you do not go into a tunnel
The machine makes buzzing and clicking sounds — this is completely normal
The whole scan takes only 5 to 15 minutes
prepare: Remove all metal items — jewellery, belts, hair clips, underwire bras
Wear loose, comfortable clothing — you may be given a hospital gown
Tell the radiographer if you are pregnant or think you might be
If you are having contrast dye — do not eat for 4 hours before your scan
breathhold: You will hear a recorded voice say: breathe in, hold your breath
Hold completely still for 5 to 10 seconds — do not breathe or swallow
Then you will hear: breathe normally — and you can relax
Practise this now using the breath trainer on the next screen
contrast: A clear dye is injected into a vein in your arm through a small needle
You will feel a warm spreading sensation through your body — this is normal
You may feel like you need to urinate — this feeling passes quickly
Tell the radiographer immediately if you feel itchy, short of breath, or sick
staying-still: Even the smallest movement blurs the images and ruins the scan
Blurry images mean the scan must be repeated — more time and more radiation
Rest your arms on your sides or on your stomach — whichever is more comfortable
Close your eyes, breathe slowly, and try to relax your whole body

breathhold.title: Breath-Hold Practice
breathhold.subtitle: Practise before you enter the scanner room
breathhold.instruction: Follow the circle. Breathe with it.
breathhold.completeSub: You can now follow the breath-hold command during your scan
breathhold.tip: During your real scan the radiographer will give you the same commands

scannerSound.title: Scanner Sound Preview
scannerSound.subtitle: Listen to the sounds the CT scanner makes
scannerSound.description: The CT scanner can be noisy. It makes buzzing, clicking, and whirring sounds while it takes pictures. Listening to these sounds now can help you feel more comfortable in the scanning room.

contrastGuide.title: Contrast Dye
contrastGuide.subtitle: What to expect if you are having contrast
contrastGuide.intro: Some CT scans use a clear liquid called contrast dye to help certain organs show up more clearly on the images.
contrastGuide.howText: It is injected into a vein in your arm through a small needle by the radiographer.
contrastGuide.feelsText: You may feel a warm flush spreading through your body, and a metallic taste in your mouth. You might also feel like you need to urinate. This is completely normal and passes very quickly.
contrastGuide.importantText: Always tell the radiographer if you feel itchy, short of breath, or unwell after the injection.
